item.title: End to repression on women demanded
item.description: Politicians and journalists at a discussion meeting in Dhaka on Sunday expressed concern over the recent increase in repression of women in the country....

item.title: Early diagnosis of kidney diseases stressed
item.description: Kidney experts stressed early diagnosis of kidney diseases, adding that, otherwise, it would be fatal for the patient....Geographic Reference

item.title: NSU hosts ‘Her Leadership, Her Legacy’
item.description: North South University’s Career and Placement Centre hosted a seminar titled Her Leadership, Her Legacy: Shaping Careers, Shaping Tomorrow, marking International Women’s Day, at the NSU in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: Violence against women cannot be solved by making laws alone: PrSTU VC
item.description: Vice-chancellor of Pirojpur Science and Technology University Professor Md Shahidul Islam on Sunday said that the incidents of violence and discrimination against women were still worrying and these problems could not be solved by making laws alone....

item.title: GAU reviews BS programme self-assessment results
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University on Sunday organised a workshop titled Sharing the Self-Assessment Survey Results of the BS (Agriculture) Programme aimed at enhancing academic quality and advancing toward international accreditation standards....

item.title: Joint drive rescues 32 people at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard and the Rapid Action Battalion in a joint drive rescued 32 people, including women and children, held captive for the purpose of smuggling to Malaysia, at Baharchhara under Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Sunday....

item.title: Agriculture secretary visits BARI
item.description: Agriculture secretary Rafiqul I Mohamed visited Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute on Saturday....

item.title: Interim govt blamed for keeping Info Commission ineffective
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh executive director Iftekharuzzaman on Sunday said that the Information Commission had been rendered ineffective during the Awami League regime, while the interim government did not make it functional despite having the opportunity....

item.title: President terms Zia proclaimer of independence
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin on Sunday termed Ziaur Rahman, former president and founder of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, the proclaimer of the country’s independence....

item.title: Three killed in road accidents in districts
item.description: At least three people —a minor boy, a college student and a truck helper — were killed in separate road accidents in Narail, Gopalganj and Madaripur districts on Sunday....

item.title: BRAC Univ holds memorial honouring Salehuddin
item.description: BRAC University has organised a memorial in honour of Salehuddin Ahmed, who served as pro-vice-chancellor from 2005 to 2009 and was formerly deputy executive director of BRAC and managing editor of The Daily Star, on its Merul Badda campus in Dhaka recently....

item.title: Hasina trying to destabilise country from India: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government minister and BNP secretary general Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Sunday alleged that former prime minister Sheikh Hasina was trying to destabilise Bangladesh while staying in India....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Iran presses Gulf attacks
item.description: Iran struck Gulf infrastructure on Sunday, hitting fuel tanks at Kuwait’s international airport and damaging a desalination plant in Bahrain as Tehran pressed its missile and drone campaign against its neighbours into a second week....

item.title: HC rejects bail pleas of nine Bawms as ‘not pressed’
item.description: The High Court on Sunday rejected the bail petitions of nine individuals belonging to the Bawm national minority community, arrested in connection with anti-terrorism cases linked to a bank robbery in Bandarban in 2024....

item.title: Washington’s imperial play: strike amid talks
item.description: Early on February 28, US and Israeli missiles struck Tehran even as negotiations between Iran and the United States were still underway, with the next round of talks scheduled for March 2 in Geneva. Launching a military assault while maintaining the façade of diplomacy was nothing less than a calculated act of deception. The attack blatantly violated international law......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Post-uprising toxic masculinity
item.description: FOR the last two years, we have been witnessing the popular rise of a type of masculine identity which has rung alarm bells in feminist and women’s activist circles. This very rigid model of masculinity is seen proliferating in public spaces, including and especially social media. The faces of the ‘July boys’ have often promoted and embodied such masculinity. Right-wing groups are at one with it......

item.title: Quiet collapse of competition
item.description: DHAKA’S traditional markets today have gone through some changes. The usual back-and-forth of bargaining isn’t there like before. Instead, prices are almost the same everywhere, and sellers don’t seem to worry about competing for customers. Take a typical afternoon at New Market or Gausia: a shopper asks the price at one stall, then walks a few steps to another......

item.title: Energy security at the mercy of global turbulence
item.description: FOR years now, the country’s energy sector has been caught in a vicious cycle. A small tremor in the global market quickly translates into domestic instability. Industrial production slows, electricity supply becomes erratic and policymakers scramble for short term solutions. What appears to be a temporary disruption is actually a structural vulnerability that has been......Daily news updates

item.title: Govt must heed women’s commission recommendations
item.description: UNABATED sexual violence and a growing insecurity show that no progress has been made in eliminating gender discrimination and violence. The Bangladesh Women’s Association has also recorded a significant increase in rape and sexual violence in 2025. Women’spoliticalparticipation has declined, with only seven being elected lawmakers. The Bangladesh Nationalist Party......

item.title: March 7 was not a mere partisan event
item.description: THE government of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Saturday arrested a few citizens, men and women, for their attempt to observe the anniversary of the historic ‘public speech’ of the country’s founding president Sheikh Mujibur Rahman delivered on March 7, 1971. The arrested people, understandably, belong to the Awami League, the activities of which were......

item.title: Books on AI on high demand
item.description: Publishers and writers said on Sunday that the demand for artificial intelligence-related publications is rising among academic, professional, and common readers....

item.title: Govt to take initiative to export food to Japan: minister
item.description: Food, fisheries and livestock and agriculture minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid on Sunday said that Bangladesh would enhance its capacity and take initiatives to export food items to the Japanese market....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Section 144 imposed over Jhenaidah BNP iftar events
item.description: THE local administration has imposed Section 144 in Kaliganj upazila of Jhenaidah after two rival groups of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party announced separate iftar gatherings at the same venue, raising concerns of a possible clash....

item.title: 3 jailed for killing 20 dogs
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday sentenced three men two and a half years in jail in a case filed under the Animal Welfare Act, 2019, over killing of 20 dogs in the city’s Dhalpur area....

item.title: UK iterates pledges support for Rohingyas
item.description: The United Kingdom on Sunday reaffirmed its commitment to continue assistance for Rohingya refugees, forcibly displaced Myanmar nationals, in Bangladesh....

item.title: Journalist couple Naem-Farida’s 22 accounts frozen
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday ordered to freeze 22 bank accounts of former editor of daily Bangladesh Pratidin, Naem Nizam and his wife former president of National Press Club and former reserved-seat lawmaker Farida Yasmin, and their related interests....Top news summaries

item.title: 57 sued over death of Jamaat activist in Rajshahi
item.description: At least 57 people, mostly activists of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, have been sued in a case filed on Sunday over the death of a Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami activist during a village meeting in Mohonpur upazila of Rajshahi....

item.title: Explosion rocks US embassy in Oslo
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion in the early hours of Sunday that caused no injuries and only ‘minor material damage’, police in the Norwegian capital said as they searched for the perpetrators....

item.title: Mugging suspect lynched in Narayanganj
item.description: A suspected mugger was lynched at Jalkuri Koroitala in Siddhirganj of Narayanganj on Sunday....

item.title: Centrist RSP heads for landslide
item.description: Nepal’s centrist RSP party of rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah had secured a majority in the direct parliamentary elections, partial official results showed Sunday, and was heading for a landslide according to official trends....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Youth hacked to death in Kushtia
item.description: A YOUNG man was hacked to death by unidentified assailants in broad daylight at sadar upazila in Kushtia on Sunday....

item.title: 28 more ME flights cancelled
item.description: At least 28 more Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from the Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city on Sunday, raising the total number of flights cancelled to 320 in 10 days from February 28 to March 9....
